overview.short: Gajpanth (also spelled Gajpantha) is a Digambar Jain pilgrimage site (Siddha-kshetra) located in Mhasrul village, in the Nashik district of the Indian state of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Gajpanth (also spelled Gajpantha) is a Digambar Jain pilgrimage site (Siddha-kshetra) located in Mhasrul village, in the Nashik district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is managed and maintained by Registered Trust No.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Niphad
topPlaces.description: Niphad is the name both of the town and the Taluka headquartered there. It is within the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Pimapalas
topPlaces.description: Pimpalas is the name both of a town and a taluka in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India. The Marathi name signifies "a place without mountains", and indeed the taluka's topography is fairly level, with hardly any hills.

topPlaces.name: Nashik division
topPlaces.description: Nashik Division is one of the six divisions of India's Maharashtra state, and four of its five districts form Uttar Maharashtra. The three northernmost districts of Uttar Maharashtra form most of the historic Khandesh region, which covers the northern part of the division in the valley of the Tapti River.

topPlaces.name: Kalaram Temple
topPlaces.description: Kalaram Temple, is a Hindu temple in Nashik of Nashik district in Maharashtra, dedicated to the deity Rama. It is a temple situated in Panchavati area of the Nashik.

topPlaces.name: Nashik
topPlaces.description: Nashik, formerly Nasik, is a city in the northern region of the Indian state of Maharashtra situated on the banks of the river Godavari, about 165 km (103 mi) northeast of the state capital Mumbai. The city lies in the Nashik Metropolitan Region.

topPlaces.name: Kapila River (Maharashtra)
topPlaces.description: The Kapila (Marathi: कपिला नदी) is a river in Maharashtra. It is a minor tributary of the Godavari River, India's second longest river after the Ganga.

topPlaces.name: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul
topPlaces.description: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul is in Nashik city, Dwarka area. It starts from Pandavleni caves and ends near Adgaon.

topPlaces.name: Hutatma Anant Kanhere Maidan
topPlaces.description: Anant Kanhere Maidan is a cricket ground in Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Formerly the stadium was known as Golf Club Ground.

items.note: Niphad is the name both of the town and the Taluka headquartered there. It is within the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India.

Pimpalas is the name both of a town and a taluka in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, India. The Marathi name signifies "a place without mountains", and indeed the taluka's topography is fairly level, with hardly any hills.

Nashik Division is one of the six divisions of India's Maharashtra state, and four of its five districts form Uttar Maharashtra. The three northernmost districts of Uttar Maharashtra form most of the historic Khandesh region, which covers the northern part of the division in the valley of the Tapti River.
Kalaram Temple, is a Hindu temple in Nashik of Nashik district in Maharashtra, dedicated to the deity Rama. It is a temple situated in Panchavati area of the Nashik.

Nashik, formerly Nasik, is a city in the northern region of the Indian state of Maharashtra situated on the banks of the river Godavari, about 165 km (103 mi) northeast of the state capital Mumbai. The city lies in the Nashik Metropolitan Region.
The Kapila (Marathi: कपिला नदी) is a river in Maharashtra. It is a minor tributary of the Godavari River, India's second longest river after the Ganga.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul & around

items.note: Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Uddanpul is in Nashik city, Dwarka area. It starts from Pandavleni caves and ends near Adgaon.
Anant Kanhere Maidan is a cricket ground in Nashik, Maharashtra, India. Formerly the stadium was known as Golf Club Ground.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gajpanth?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gajpanth is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (74 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gajpanth?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gajpanth at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gajpanth?
faq.a: Stays in Gajpanth start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gajpanth?
faq.a: The top attractions are Niphad, Pimapalas, Nashik division, Kalaram Temple, Nashik.
